paper.title: Use of fine glass as ASR inhibitor in glass aggregate mortars
paper.authors: Rachida Idir; Martin Cyr; Arezki Tagnit-Hamou
paper.material_system: Glass aggregate mortars for ASR testing
paper.notes: Compressive-strength results are described as plotted in Fig. 1; exact values are not available in the supplied packet.

strength_results.notes: Fig. 1 includes compressive-strength results for mortars containing different glass size classes; values require digitization and mixture mapping.

conditions.curing_method: demoulded after 24 h; stored at 60 °C in watertight containers above 20 mm water; cooled 24 h at 20 °C before measurements

figures.caption: Fig. 1. Expansion and compressive-strength results of mortars containing different glass size classes.

evidence.evidence_text: Study of fine glass as ASR inhibitor in glass aggregate mortars; mechanical tests on mortar samples were used for pozzolanic assessment.
Mortars were cast in 2 x 2 x 16 cm prisms, demolded after 24 h, stored at 60 °C in watertight containers, and measurements were made after cooling.
Fig. 1 gives compressive strength results of mortars containing different size classes of glass; strengths were up to 10 MPa lower for C0 and C1 and fines C3-C8 gave extra strength.

review_queue.question: Digitize Fig. 1 compressive-strength values and map each glass class/replacement mixture.
review_queue.suggested_action: Use figure digitization or source table if available; do not insert until mixture labels and values are confirmed.

main_risks: No exact compressive-strength table is present in the supplied packet; values require figure digitization.
